## Get charge summary details by charge ID

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-charge-revenue-summaries-subscription-charges-by-charge-key>`

This REST API reference describes how to retrieve the details of a charge revenue summary by
specifying the subscription charge ID. This response retrieves all revenue items associated with a
charge revenue summary. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/charge-revenue-summaries/subscription-charges/{charge-key} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Charge key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the subscription rate plan charge; for example, 402892793e173340013e173b81000012.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get Credit Memos

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-creditmemos>`

Retrieves the information about all credit memos.

The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes Unapplied
Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support.

### Filtering

You can use query parameters to restrict the data returned in the response. Each query parameter
corresponds to one field in the response body. If the value of a filterable field is string, you can
set the corresponding query parameter to `null` when filtering. Then, you can get the response data
with this field value being `null`.

Examples:

- /v1/creditmemos?status=Processed
- /v1/creditmemos?referredInvoiceId=null&status=Draft
- /v1/creditmemos?status=Processed&type=External&sort=+number

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/creditmemos endpoint.

the data returned in the response. You can use this parameter to supply a dimension you want to sort on. A sortable field uses the following form: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> You can use at most two sortable fields in one URL path. Use a comma to separate sortable fields. For example: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong>, <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> <strong>operator</strong> is used to mark the order of sequencing. The operator is optional. If you only specify the sortable field without any operator, the response data is sorted in descending order by this field. - The <code>-</code> operator indicates an ascending order. - The <code>+</code> operator indicates a descending order. By default, the response data is displayed in descending order by credit memo number. <strong>field_name</strong> indicates the name of a sortable field. The supported sortable fields of this operation are as below: - accountId - amount - appliedAmount - createdById - createdDate - creditMemoDate - number - referredInvoiceId - refundAmount - status - targetDate - taxAmount - totalTaxExemptAmount - transferredToAccounting - unappliedAmount - updatedDate Examples: - /v1/creditmemos?sort=+number - /v1/creditmemos?status=Processed&amp;sort=-number,+amount</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Total Tax Exempt Amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>totalTaxExemptAmount</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about a specific credit
memo. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/creditmemos/{creditMemoId} endpoint

## Get credit Memo Items

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-creditmemos-items-by-credit-memo-id>`

The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes Unapplied
Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about all items of a credit
memo. A credit memo item is a single line item in a credit memo.

### Filtering

You can use query parameters to restrict the data returned in the response. Each query parameter
corresponds to one field in the response body. If the value of a filterable field is string, you can
set the corresponding query parameter to `null` when filtering. Then, you can get the response data
with this field value being `null`.

Examples:

- /v1/creditmemos/402890245c7ca371015c7cb40ac30015/items?amount=100
- /v1/creditmemos/402890245c7ca371015c7cb40ac30015/items?amount=100&sort=createdDate

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/creditmemos/{creditMemoId}/items endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Credit Memo Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a credit memo. For example, 8a8082e65b27f6c3015ba45ff82c7172.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Service End Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>serviceEndDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the <code>skuName</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source Item Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the on the <code>subscriptionId</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Applied Amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>appliedAmount</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>amount</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>id</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per <code>refundAmount</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedById</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>sort</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter restricts the order of the data returned in the response. You can use this parameter to supply a dimension you want to sort on. A sortable field uses the following form: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> You can use at most two sortable fields in one URL path. Use a comma to separate sortable fields. For example: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong>, <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> <strong>operator</strong> is used to mark the order of sequencing. The operator is optional. If you only specify the sortable field without any operator, the response data is sorted in descending order by this field. - The <code>-</code> operator indicates an ascending order. - The <code>+</code> operator indicates a descending order. By default, the response data is displayed in descending order by updated date. <strong>field_name</strong> indicates the name of a sortable field. The supported sortable fields of this operation are as below: - amount - appliedAmount - createdById - createdDate - id - refundAmount - serviceEndDate - serviceStartDate - sku - skuName - sourceItemId - subscriptionId - updatedById - updatedDate Examples: - /v1/creditmemos/402890245c7ca371015c7cb40ac30015/items?sort=createdDate - /v1/creditmemos/402890245c7ca371015c7cb40ac30015/items?amount=100&amp;sort=createdDate</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The minor version of the Zuora REST API. See [Minor Version](<a href="https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/">https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/</a>) for information about REST API version control. This header affects the availability of the following response fields: * <code>items</code> &gt; <code>creditTaxItems</code> * <code>items</code> &gt; <code>taxationItems</code> * <code>items</code> &gt; <code>comment</code> * <code>items</code> &gt; <code>description</code></p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. ## Get credit memo item

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-creditmemos-items-by-credit-memo-id-cmitemid>`

The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes Unapplied
Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support.

Retrieves the information about a specific item of a credit memo. A credit memo item is a single
line item in a credit memo. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/creditmemos/{creditMemoId}/items/{cmitemid} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>cmitemid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a credit memo item. You can get the credit memo item ID from the response of [Get credit memo items](<a href="https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/">https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/</a>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Credit Memo Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a credit memo. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Get credit memo parts

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-creditmemos-parts-by-credit-memo-id>`

**Note:** The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes
Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about all parts of a
credit memo. A credit memo can consist of an unapplied part, and several parts applied to invoices
and debit memos. You can use this operation to get all the applied and unapplied portions of a
credit memo. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/creditmemos/{creditMemoId}/parts
endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Credit Memo Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a credit memo. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Get credit memo part

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-creditmemos-parts-by-credit-memo-id-partid>`

**Note:** The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes
Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about a specific credit
memo part. A credit memo can consist of an unapplied part, and several parts applied to invoices and
debit memos. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/creditmemos/{creditMemoId}/parts/{partid} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>partid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a specific credit memo part. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Get credit memo part items

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-creditmemos-parts-itemparts-by-credit-memo-id-partid>`

**Note:** The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes
Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about all items of a
credit memo part. A credit memo part item is a single line item in a credit memo part. A credit memo
part can consist of several different types of items. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /v1/creditmemos/{creditMemoId}/parts/{partid}/itemparts endpoint

## Get custom foreign currency exchange rates

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-custom-exchange-rates-by-currency>`

This feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature, submit a request to
Zuora Global Support. This reference describes how to query custom foreign exchange rates from
Zuora. You can use this API method to query exchange rates only if you use a custom exchange rate
provider and upload rates with the Import Foreign Exchange Rates mass action. This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /v1/custom-exchange-rates/{currency} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>currency</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The target base currency of the tenant. The exchange rates in the response are calculated in relation to the target currency. The value must be a three-letter currency code, for example, USD.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Start date of the date range for which you want to get exchange rates. The date must be in yyyy-mm-dd format, for example, 2016-01-15. The start date cannot be later than the end date.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>End Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>End date of the date range for which you want to get exchange rates. The date must be in yyyy-mm-dd format, for example, 2016-01-16. The end date can be a maximum of 90 days after the start date.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Get Debit Memos

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-debitmemos>`

Retrieves the information about all debit memos associated with all customer accounts.

**Note:** The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes
Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support.

### Filtering

You can use query parameters to restrict the data returned in the response. Each query parameter
corresponds to one field in the response body. If the value of a filterable field is string, you can
set the corresponding query parameter to `null` when filtering. Then, you can get the response data
with this field value being `null`.

Examples:

- /v1/debitmemos?status=Processed
- /v1/debitmemos?referredInvoiceId=null&status=Draft
- /v1/debitmemos?status=Processed&type=External&sort=+number

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/debitmemos endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>targetDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>number</code> of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DRAFT</p></li><li><p>POSTED</p></li><li><p>CANCELED</p></li><li><p>ERROR</p></li><li><p>PENDING_FOR_TAX</p></li><li><p>GENERATING</p></li><li><p>CANCEL_IN_PROGRESS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>This response based on the <code>currency</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedById</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>sort</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter restricts the order of the data returned in the response. You can use this parameter to supply a dimension you want to sort on. A sortable field uses the following form: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> You can use at most two sortable fields in one URL path. Use a comma to separate sortable fields. For example: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong>, <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> <strong>operator</strong> is used to mark the order of sequencing. The operator is optional. If you only specify the sortable field without any operator, the response data is sorted in descending order by this field. - The <code>-</code> operator indicates an ascending order. - The <code>+</code> operator indicates a descending order. By default, the response data is displayed in descending order by debit memo number. <strong>field_name</strong> indicates the name of a sortable field. The supported sortable fields of this operation are as below: - number - accountId - debitMemoDate - targetDate - dueDate - amount - taxAmount - totalTaxExemptAmount - balance - beAppliedAmount - referredInvoiceId - createdDate - createdById - updatedDate - updatedById Examples: - /v1/debitmemos?sort=+number - /v1/debitmemos?status=Processed&amp;sort=-number,+amount</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Total Tax Exempt Amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>totalTaxExemptAmount</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Get debit memo application parts

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-debitmemos-application-parts-by-debit-memo-id>`

This feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes Unapplied Payments, Credit and
Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this feature, submit a request to
Zuora Global Support.

Retrieves information about the payments or credit memos that are applied to a specified debit memo.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/debitmemos/{debitMemoId}/application-parts
endpoint.

## Get Debit Memo

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-debitmemos-by-debit-memo-id>`

This feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes Unapplied Payments, Credit and
Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this feature, submit a request to
Zuora Global Support.

Retrieves the information about a specific debit memo.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/debitmemos/{debitMemoId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Debit Memo Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a debit memo. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Get Debit Memo Items

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-debitmemos-items-by-debit-memo-id>`

**Note:** The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes
Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support.

Retrieves the information about all items of a debit memo. A debit memo item is a single line item
in a debit memo.

### Filtering

You can use query parameters to restrict the data returned in the response. Each query parameter
corresponds to one field in the response body. If the value of a filterable field is string, you can
set the corresponding query parameter to `null` when filtering. Then, you can get the response data
with this field value being `null`.

Examples:

- /v1/debitmemos/402890245c7ca371015c7cb40b28001f/items?amount=100
- /v1/debitmemos/402890245c7ca371015c7cb40b28001f/items?amount=100&sort=createdDate

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/debitmemos/{debitMemoId}/items endpoint.

### Parameters

page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedById</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>sort</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter restricts the order of the data returned in the response. You can use this parameter to supply a dimension you want to sort on. A sortable field uses the following form: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> You can use at most two sortable fields in one URL path. Use a comma to separate sortable fields. For example: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong>, <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> <strong>operator</strong> is used to mark the order of sequencing. The operator is optional. If you only specify the sortable field without any operator, the response data is sorted in descending order by this field. - The <code>-</code> operator indicates an ascending order. - The <code>+</code> operator indicates a descending order. By default, the response data is displayed in descending order by updated date. <strong>field_name</strong> indicates the name of a sortable field. The supported sortable fields of this operation are as below: - id - amount - beAppliedAmount - sku - skuName - serviceStartDate - serviceEndDate - sourceItemId - createdDate - createdById - updatedDate - updatedById - subscriptionId Examples: - /v1/debitmemos/402890245c7ca371015c7cb40b28001f/items?sort=createdDate - /v1/debitmemos/402890245c7ca371015c7cb40b28001f/items?amount=100&amp;sort=createdDate</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. - MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:UNAUTHORIZED
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get taxation items of debit memo item

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-debitmemos-items-taxation-items-by-debit-memo-id-dmitemid>`

**Note:** The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes
Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves information about the taxation items of
a specific debit memo item. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/debitmemos/{debitMemoId}/items/{dmitemid}/taxation-items endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>dmitemid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a debit memo item. ## Describe object

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-describe-by-object>`

Provides a reference listing for each object that is available in your Zuora tenant. The information
returned by this call is useful if you are using
[CRUD: Create Export](https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/) or the AQuA API to
create a data source export.

### Response

The response contains an XML document that lists the fields of the specified object. Each of the
objectâs fields is represented by an element in the XML document. Each field element contains the
following elements:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Element</p></td><td><p>Description</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>&lt;name&gt;</code></p></td><td><p>API name of the field.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>&lt;label&gt;</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the field in the Zuora user interface.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>&lt;type&gt;</code></p></td><td><p>Data type of the field. The possible data types are: <code>boolean</code>, <code>date</code>, <code>datetime</code>, <code>decimal</code>, <code>integer</code>, <code>picklist</code>, <code>text</code>, <code>timestamp</code>, and <code>ZOQL</code>. If the data type is <code>picklist</code>, the <code>&lt;field&gt;</code> element contains an <code>&lt;options&gt;</code> element that lists the possible values of the field.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>&lt;contexts&gt;</code></p></td><td><p>Specifies the availability of the field. If the <code>&lt;contexts&gt;</code> element lists the <code>export</code> context, the field is available for use in data source exports.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

The `<field>` element contains other elements that provide legacy information about the field. This
information is not directly related to the REST API.

Response sample:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<object>
  <name>ProductRatePlanCharge</name>
  <label>Product Rate Plan Charge</label>
  <fields>
    ...
    <field>
      <name>TaxMode</name>
      <label>Tax Mode</label>
      <type>picklist</type>
      <options>
        <option>TaxExclusive</option>
        <option>TaxInclusive</option>
      </options>
      <contexts>
        <context>export</context>
      </contexts>
      ...
    </field>
    ...
  </fields>
</object>
```

It is strongly recommended that your integration checks `<contexts>` elements in the response. If
your integration does not check `<contexts>` elements, your integration may process fields that are
not available for use in data source exports. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/describe/{object} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>object</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API name of an object in your Zuora tenant. For example, <code>InvoiceItem</code>. See [Zuora Object Model](<a href="https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/">https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/</a>) for the list of valid object names. Depending on the features enabled in your Zuora tenant, you may not be able to list the fields of some objects.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get document properties

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-document-properties-by-document-type-document-id>`

This feature is available only if you have the Billing Document Properties Setup feature enabled.
The Billing Document Properties Setup feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to
this feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support.

You can retrieve information about document properties of a billing document. Billing documents
include invoices, credit memos, and debit memos. **Note:** You can retrieve information about
document properties of credit and debit memos only if you have the Invoice Settlement feature
enabled. The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/document-properties/{documentType}/{documentId}
endpoint.

## Multi-entity: Get entities

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-entities>`

The Multi-entity feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature, submit
a request to Zuora Global Support.

Retrieves detailed information of certain entities in a multi-entity hierarchy.

You can retrieve:

- Provisioned entities
- Unprovisioned entities
- Both provisioned and unprovisioned entities

### User Access Permission

You can make the call as any entity user.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/entities endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>provisioned</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specify whether to retrieve provisioned or unprovisioned entities: - <code>true</code>: Provisioned entities - <code>false</code>: Unprovisioned entities If you do not specify this field in the request, both the provisioned and unprovisioned entities are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND

## Multi-entity: Get entity by Id

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-entities-by-id>`

The Multi-entity feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature, submit
a request to Zuora Global Support.

Retrieves detailed information about a specified entity.

### User Access Permission

You can make the call as any entity user.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/entities/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specify the Id of the entity that you want to retrieve. ## Multi-entity: Get connections

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-entity-connections>`

The Multi-entity feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature, submit
a request to Zuora Global Support.

Retrieves information about certain connections for a specified entity. You can specify the entity
to retrieve in the `Zuora-Entity-Ids` request header.

You can retrieve:

- Inbound connections
- Outbound connections
- Both inbound and outbound connections

### User Access Permission

You can make the call as any entity user.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/entity-connections endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>INBOUND</p></li><li><p>OUTBOUND</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies whether to retrieve inbound or outbound connections for an entity. Possible values: - <code>inbound</code>: All the incoming connections to the entity. - <code>outbound</code>: All the outgoing connections from the entity. If you do not specify this field in the request, both the inbound and outbound connections are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Get files

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-files-by-file-id>`

Retrieve files such as export results, invoices, and accounting period reports.

> [!NOTE] The maximum file size is 2047MB. If you have a data request that exceeds this limit, Zuora
> returns the following 403 response: `2047MB`. Submit a request to Zuora Global Support to
> determine if large file optimization is an option for you.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/files/{file-id} endpoint.

## Return Hosted Pages

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-hostedpages>`

Returns the Payment Pages configuration metadata, specifically, page ID, page version, payment
method type.

The following are the version-specific and general REST requests for Payment Pages:

- The request for Payment Pages 1.0 configuration information:  
  `GET /hostedpages?version=1`
- The request for Payment Pages 2.0 configuration information:  
  `GET /hostedpages?version=2`
- The request for all versions of Payment Pages configuration information:  
  `GET /hostedpages`

### Notes

If you do not have the corresponding tenant setting enabled, for example, the request `version`
parameter set to 2 with the Payment Pages 2.0 setting disabled, you will receive an error.

This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/hostedpages endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Version Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Version of the Payment Pages for which you want to retrieve the configuration information. Specify 1 for Payment Pages 1.0 or 2 for Payment Pages 2.0. If omitted, information for all versions of Payment Pages are returned. The response also depends on your tenant settings for Payment Pages 1.0 and Payment Pages 2.0. For example, if only the tenant setting for Payment Pages 2.0 is enabled, the response will only contain information for Payment Pages 2.0 forms even when this parameter is omitted.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get invoice application parts

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-invoices-application-parts-by-invoice-id>`

Note: The Invoice Settlement feature is in Limited Availability. This feature includes Unapplied
Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you wish to have access to the
feature, submit a request at Zuora Global Support. Retrieves information about the payments or
credit memos that are applied to a specified invoice. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /v1/invoices/{invoiceId}/application-parts endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Invoice Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of an invoice. For example, 2c92c8955bd63cc1015bd7c151af02ab.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. ## Get invoice files

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-invoices-files-by-invoice-id>`

Retrieves the information about all PDF files of a specified invoice. Invoice PDF files are returned
in reverse chronological order by the value of the `versionNumber` field. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /v1/invoices/{invoiceId}/files endpoint

This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

## Get callout notification histories

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-notification-history-callout>`

This REST API reference describes how to get a notification history for callouts. This operation
makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/notification-history/callout endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial date and time of records to be returned. Defaults to (end time - 1 day). Use format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Category</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Category of records to be returned by event category. The following formats are supported: * <code>{Event Type Namespace}:{Event Type Name}</code> if the Configurable Event features are enabled in your Zuora tenant. For example: <code>user.notification:NewSubscriptionCreated</code>. * Otherwise, numeric code of the event category. For example, <code>1210</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Failed Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, only return failed records. If <code>false</code>, return all records in the given date range. The default value is <code>true</code>.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>End Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The final date and time of records to be returned. Defaults to now. Use format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of an object that triggered a callout notification.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Response Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Include Response Content</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:BAD_REQUEST
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
