## Get email notification histories

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-notification-history-email>`

This REST API reference describes how to get a notification history for notification emails. ##
Notes Request parameters and their values may be appended with a "?" following the HTTPS GET
request. Additional request parameter are separated by "&". For example:
`GET [https://rest.zuora.com/v1/notification-history/email?startTime=2015-01-12T00:00:00&endTime=2015-01-15T00:00:00&failedOnly=false&eventCategory=1000&pageSize=1](https://rest.zuora.com/v1/notification-history/email?startTime=2015-01-12T00:00:00&endTime=2015-01-15T00:00:00&failedOnly=false&eventCategory=1000&pageSize=1)`
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/notification-history/email endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial date and time of records to be returned. Defaults to (end time - 1 day). Use format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss. The maximum date range (endTime - startTime) is three days.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Category</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Category of records to be returned by event category. The following formats are supported: * <code>{Event Type Namespace}:{Event Type Name}</code> if the Configurable Event features are enabled in your Zuora tenant. For example: <code>user.notification:NewSubscriptionCreated</code>. * Otherwise, numeric code of the event category. For example, <code>1210</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Failed Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, only returns failed records. When <code>false</code>, returns all records in the given date range. Defaults to <code>true</code> when not specified.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>End Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The end date and time of records to be returned. Defaults to now. Use format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss. The maximum date range (endTime - startTime) is three days.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The Id of an object that triggered an email notification.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## CRUD: Get bill run

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-object-bill-run-by-id>`

**Note:** This feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you wish to have access to the feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Business operations depending on the completion of the
bill run will not be available while the bill run query returns `PostInProgress`. Upon completion of
the bill run, a query will return `Posted`. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/object/bill-run/{id} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Object id</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Object fields to return</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## CRUD: Retrieve Export

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-object-export-by-id>`

Use this operation to check the status of a data source export and access the exported data. When
you export data from Zuora, each exported file is available for download for 7 days. Data source
exports (Export objects) older than 90 days are automatically deleted. This operation makes an HTTP
GET request to the /v1/object/export/{id} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Object id</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Object fields to return</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:BAD_REQUEST
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:TIMEOUT
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:UNAUTHORIZED
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

### Parameters

Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## CRUD: Get payment method snapshot

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-object-payment-method-snapshot-by-id>`

This REST API reference describes how to retrieve a Payment Method Snapshot. A Payment Method
Snapshot is a copy of the particular Payment Method used in a transaction. If the Payment Method is
deleted, the Payment Method Snapshot continues to retain the data used in each of the past
transactions. ## Notes The following Payment Method fields are not available in Payment Method
Snapshots: \* `Active` \* `AchAddress1` \* `AchAddress2` \* `CreatedById` \* `CreatedDate` \*
`UpdatedById` \* `UpdatedDate` The Payment Method Snapshot field `PaymentMethodId` is not available
in Payment Methods. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/object/payment-method-snapshot/{id} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Object id</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Object fields to return</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR

Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND

### Parameters

Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-object-unit-of-measure-by-id>`

CRUD: Retrieve UnitOfMeasure This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/object/unit-of-measure/{id} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Object id</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Object fields to return</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. ## Get all orders

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-orders>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Order Metrics feature enabled. The
migration to Order Metrics is in **Limited Availability**. We are actively soliciting feedback from
a small set of early adopters before releasing as generally available. If you have the Orders
feature enabled, you already have the Order Metrics feature enabled. Retrieves information about all
orders in your tenant. By default, it returns the first page of the orders. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /v1/orders endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>End Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The result will only contains orders with the date of dateFilterOption earlier than or equal to this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Date Filter Option</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The date type to filter on. This field value can be orderDate or updatedDate. Default is orderDate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The result will only contain the orders with the date of dateFilterOption later than or equal to this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configurations](#Config)

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND

## Get order metrics for evergreen subscription

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-orders-evergreen-metrics-by-order-number-subscription-number>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Order Metrics feature enabled. The
migration to Order Metrics is in **Limited Availability**. We are actively soliciting feedback from
a small set of early adopters before releasing as generally available. If you have the Orders
feature enabled, you already have the Order Metrics feature enabled. Retrieves the metrics of an
evergreen subscription in a specified order. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/orders/{orderNumber}/evergreenMetrics/{subscriptionNumber} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Order Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The order number.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The subscription number you want to get the metrics for.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The start date of the date range for which you want to get the metrics. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. `<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-orders-subscription-owner-by-account-number>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Order Metrics feature enabled. The
migration to Order Metrics is in **Limited Availability**. We are actively soliciting feedback from
a small set of early adopters before releasing as generally available. If you have the Orders
feature enabled, you already have the Order Metrics feature enabled. Retrieves the detailed
information about all orders for a specified subscription owner. Any orders containing the changes
on the subscriptions owned by this account are returned. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /v1/orders/subscriptionOwner/{accountNumber} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The subscription owner account number.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>End Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The result will only contain the orders with the date of 'dateFilterOption' earlier than or equal to this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Date Filter Option</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The date type to filter on. This field value can be 'orderDate' or 'updatedDate'. Default is orderDate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The result will only contain the orders with the date of 'dateFilterOption' later than or equal to this date.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get term information for subscription

a small set of early adopters before releasing as generally available. If you have the Orders
feature enabled, you already have the Order Metrics feature enabled. Retrieves the terms of the
specified subscription. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/orders/term/{subscriptionNumber} endpoint

### Parameters

## Get credit card payment methods for account

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-payment-methods-credit-cards-accounts-by-account-key>`

This REST API reference describes how to retrieve all credit card information for the specified
customer account. ## Notes The response includes details of credit or debit cards for the specified
customer account. Card numbers are masked, e.g., "\*\*1234". Cards are returned in reverse
chronological order of last update. You can send requests for bank transfer or ACH type payment
methods. However, the response will not include effective details of these payment methods. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/payment-methods/credit-cards/accounts/{account-key}
endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Account number or account ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Get stored credential profiles

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-payment-methods-profiles-by-payment-method-id>`

Retrieves the stored credential profiles within a payment method. **Note:** This feature is in
**Limited Availability**. We are actively soliciting feedback from a small set of early adopters
before releasing as generally available. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/payment-methods/{payment-method-id}/profiles endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payment method id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of a payment method.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include All</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies whether to retrieve all the stored credential profiles within the payment method. By default, Zuora returns only the stored credential profiles with <code>Agreed</code> or <code>Active</code> status. If you set this parameter to <code>true</code>, Zuora returns all the stored credential profiles.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get payment runs

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-payment-runs>`

Retrieves the information about all payment runs. You can define filterable fields to restrict the
data returned in the response. # Filtering You can use query parameters to restrict the data
returned in the response. Each query parameter corresponds to one field in the response body. If the
value of a filterable field is string, you can set the corresponding query parameter to `null` when
filtering. Then, you can get the response data with this field value being `null`. Examples: -
/v1/payment-runs?status=Processed - /v1/payment-runs?targetDate=2017-10-10&status=Pending -
/v1/payment-runs?status=Completed&sort=+updatedDate This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/payment-runs endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>targetDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdById</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>status</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PENDING</p></li><li><p>PROCESSING</p></li><li><p>COMPLETED</p></li><li><p>ERROR</p></li><li><p>CANCELED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>status</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedById</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>sort</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter restricts the order of the data returned in the response. You can use this parameter to supply a dimension you want to sort on. A sortable field uses the following form: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> You can use at most two sortable fields in one URL path. Use a comma to separate sortable fields. For example: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong>, <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> <strong>operator</strong> is used to mark the order of sequencing. The operator is optional. If you only specify the sortable field without any operator, the response data is sorted in descending order by this field. - The <code>-</code> operator indicates an ascending order. - The <code>+</code> operator indicates a descending order. By default, the response data is displayed in descending order by payment run number. <strong>field_name</strong> indicates the name of a sortable field. The supported sortable fields of this operation are as below: - targetDate - status - createdDate - createdById - updatedDate - updatedById Examples: - /v1/payment-runs?sort=+createdDate - /v1/payment-runs?status=Processing&amp;sort=-createdById,+targetDate</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR

## Get all payments

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-payments>`

**Note:** The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes
Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about all payments from
all your customer accounts. # Filtering You can use query parameters to restrict the data returned
in the response. Each query parameter corresponds to one field in the response body. If the value of
a filterable field is string, you can set the corresponding query parameter to `null` when
filtering. Then, you can get the response data with this field value being `null`. Examples: -
/v1/payments?status=Processed - /v1/payments?currency=USD&status=Processed -
/v1/payments?status=Processed&type=External&sort=+number This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /v1/payments endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Unapplied Amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>unappliedAmount</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>number</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdById</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Applied Amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>appliedAmount</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>accountId</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Credit Balance Amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on parameter filters the response based on the <code>status</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Effective Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>effectiveDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>currency</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>currency</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Refund Amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>refundAmount</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedById</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>sort</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter restricts the order of the data returned in the response. You can use this parameter to supply a dimension you want to sort on. A sortable field uses the following form: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> You can use at most two sortable fields in one URL path. Use a comma to separate sortable fields. For example: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong>, <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> <strong>operator</strong> is used to mark the order of sequencing. The operator is optional. If you only specify the sortable field without any operator, the response data is sorted in descending order by this field. - The <code>-</code> operator indicates an ascending order. - The <code>+</code> operator indicates a descending order. By default, the response data is displayed in descending order by payment number. <strong>field_name</strong> indicates the name of a sortable field. The supported sortable fields of this operation are as below: - number - accountId - amount - appliedAmount - unappliedAmount - refundAmount - creditBalanceAmount - effectiveDate - createdDate - createdById - updatedDate - updatedById Examples: - /v1/payments?sort=+number - /v1/payments?status=Processed&amp;sort=-number,+amount</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>EXTERNAL</p></li><li><p>ELECTRONIC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>type</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Get payment

Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about one specific
payment. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/payments/{paymentId} endpoint

## Get payment parts

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-payments-parts-by-payment-id>`

**Note:** The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes
Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about all parts of a
payment. A payment can consist of an unapplied part, and several parts applied to invoices and debit
memos. You can use this operation to get all the applied and unapplied portions of a payment. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/payments/{paymentId}/parts endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a payment.
