If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get payment part

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-payments-parts-by-payment-id-partid>`

**Note:** The Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. This feature includes
Unapplied Payments, Credit and Debit Memo, and Invoice Item Settlement. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about a specific
payment part. A payment can consist of an unapplied part, and several parts applied to invoices and
debit memos. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/payments/{paymentId}/parts/{partid}
endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>partid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a specific payment part. You can get the payment part ID from the response of [Get payment parts](<a href="https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/">https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/</a>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a payment. For example, 8a8082e65b27f6c3015b89e4344c16b1.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Get payment part items

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-payments-parts-itemparts-by-payment-id-partid>`

**Note:** The Invoice Item Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**, and it must be used
together with other Invoice Settlement features (Unapplied Payments, and Credit and Debit memos). If
you want access to this feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information
about all items of a payment part. A payment part item is a single line item in a payment part. A
payment part can consist of several different types of items. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /v1/payments/{paymentId}/parts/{partid}/itemparts endpoint

## Get product rate plans

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-rateplan-product-rate-plan-by-product-id>`

Retrieves information about all product rate plans of a specific product. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /v1/rateplan/{product_id}/productRatePlan endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Product id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a product. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Get all refunds

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-refunds>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled. The
Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about all refunds. Two types of
refunds are available, electronic refunds and external refunds. # Filtering You can use query
parameters to restrict the data returned in the response. Each query parameter corresponds to one
field in the response body. If the value of a filterable field is string, you can set the
corresponding query parameter to `null` when filtering. Then, you can get the response data with
this field value being `null`. Examples: - /v1/refunds?status=Processed -
/v1/refunds?amount=4&status=Processed - /v1/refunds?status=Processed&type=External&sort=+number This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/refunds endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>number</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdById</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>paymentId</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>accountId</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>amount</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>amount</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>PROCESSED</p></li><li><p>CANCELED</p></li><li><p>ERROR</p></li><li><p>PROCESSING</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>status</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated By Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedById</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Refund Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>refundDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>sort</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter restricts the order of the data returned in the response. You can use this parameter to supply a dimension you want to sort on. A sortable field uses the following form: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> You can use at most two sortable fields in one URL path. Use a comma to separate sortable fields. For example: <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong>, <strong>operator</strong> <strong>field_name</strong> <strong>operator</strong> is used to mark the order of sequencing. The operator is optional. If you only specify the sortable field without any operator, the response data is sorted in descending order by this field. - The <code>-</code> operator indicates an ascending order. - The <code>+</code> operator indicates a descending order. By default, the response data is displayed in descending order by refund number. <strong>field_name</strong> indicates the name of a sortable field. The supported sortable fields of this operation are as below: - number - accountId - amount - refundDate - paymentId - createdDate - createdById - updatedDate - updatedById Examples: - /v1/refunds?sort=+number - /v1/refunds?status=Processed&amp;sort=-number,+amount</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>EXTERNAL</p></li><li><p>ELECTRONIC</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>type</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updated Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>updatedDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created Date</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter filters the response based on the <code>createdDate</code> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Get refund parts

The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR

## Get refund part

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-refunds-parts-by-refund-id-refundpartid>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled. The
Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about a specific refund part.
This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/refunds/{refundId}/parts/{refundpartid} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>refundpartid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a specific refund part. You can get the refund part ID from the response of [Get refund parts](<a href="https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/">https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/</a>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Refund Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a refund. For example, 4028905f5a87c0ff015a889e590e00c9.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. ## Get refund part items

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-refunds-parts-itemparts-by-refund-id-refundpartid>`

**Note:** The Invoice Item Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to
this feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the information about all items of
a refund part. A refund part item is a single line item in a refund part. A refund part can consist
of several different types of items. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/refunds/{refundId}/parts/{refundpartid}/itemparts endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>refundpartid</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a specific refund part. You can get the refund part ID from the response of [Get refund parts](<a href="https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/">https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/</a>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Refund Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a refund. For example, 4028905f5a87c0ff015a889e590e00c9.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. ## Get revenue event details

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-revenue-events-by-event-number>`

This REST API reference describes how to get revenue event details by specifying the revenue event
number. Request and response field descriptions and sample code are provided. This operation makes
an HTTP GET request to the /v1/revenue-events/{event-number} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number associated with the revenue event.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

The revenue schedule number is always prefixed with "RS", for example, "RS-00000001".</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>300</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get revenue recognition rule by product rate plan charge

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-revenue-recognition-rules-product-charges-by-charge-key>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled. The
Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the revenue recognition rule associated with a
production rate plan charge by specifying the charge ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /v1/revenue-recognition-rules/product-charges/{charge-key} endpoint

The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Get revenue schedule by credit memo item ID

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-revenue-schedules-credit-memo-items-by-cmi-id>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled. The
Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the details about a revenue schedule by
specifying a valid credit memo item ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/revenue-schedules/credit-memo-items/{cmi-id} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cmi id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a credit memo item. You can get the credit memo item ID from the response of [Get credit memo items](<a href="https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/">https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/</a>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get revenue schedule by debit memo item ID

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-revenue-schedules-debit-memo-items-by-dmi-id>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled. The
Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the details about a revenue schedule by
specifying a valid debit memo item ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/revenue-schedules/debit-memo-items/{dmi-id} endpoint

## Get all revenue schedules of product charge by charge ID and billing

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-revenue-schedules-product-charges-by-charge-key-account-key>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled. The
Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves the details about all revenue schedules of a
product rate plan charge by specifying the charge ID and billing account ID. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /v1/revenue-schedules/product-charges/{charge-key}/{account-key} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The account number or account ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Charge key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a product rate plan charge. For example, 8a8082e65ba86084015bb323d3c61d82.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>8</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get revenue schedule by subscription charge

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-revenue-schedules-subscription-charges-by-charge-key>`

Retrieves the revenue schedule details by specifying subscription charge ID. Request and response
field descriptions and sample code are provided This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/revenue-schedules/subscription-charges/{charge-key} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Charge key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the subscription rate plan charge; for example, 402892793e173340013e173b81000012.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>8</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Get sequence sets

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-sequence-sets>`

Retrieves information about all sequence sets configured for billing documents, payments, and
refunds. Billing documents include invoices, credit memos, and debit memos. You can use query
parameters to restrict the data returned in the response. **Note**: The Credit and Debit Memos
feature is only available if you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled. The Invoice Settlement
feature is in Limited Availability. If you want access to this feature, submit a request to Zuora
Global Support. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /v1/sequence-sets endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of a specific sequence set.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. ## Get the revenue automation start date

field descriptions and sample code are provided. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/settings/finance/revenue-automation-start-date endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configurations](#Config)

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get subscriptions by account

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-subscriptions-accounts-by-account-key>`

Retrieves all subscriptions associated with the specified account. Zuora only returns the latest
version of the subscriptions. Subscription data is returned in reverse chronological order based on
`updatedDate`. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/subscriptions/accounts/{account-key} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Possible values are: * an account number * an account ID</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows returned per page.</p></td><td><p>20</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Charge detail</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The segmented rate plan charges. When an amendment results in a change to a charge, Zuora creates a segmented rate plan charge. Use this field to track segment charges. Possible values are: * <em>last-segment</em>: (Default) The last rate plan charge on the subscription. The last rate plan charge is the last one in the order of time on the subscription rather than the most recent changed charge on the subscription. * <em>current-segment</em>: The segmented charge that is active on todayâs date (effectiveStartDate &lt;= todayâs date &lt; effectiveEndDate). * <em>all-segments</em>: All the segmented charges. The <code>chargeSegments</code> field is returned in the response. The <code>chargeSegments</code> field contains an array of the charge information for all the charge segments. * <em>specific-segment&amp;as-of-date=date</em>: The segmented charge that is active on a date you specified (effectiveStartDate &lt;= specific date &lt; effectiveEndDate). The format of the date is yyyy-mm-dd.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Page Number</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The initial page to get in the first request</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:BAD_REQUEST
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:SERVER_ERROR

## Get subscriptions by key

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-subscriptions-by-subscription-key>`

This REST API reference describes how to retrieve detailed information about a specified
subscription in the latest version. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/subscriptions/{subscription-key} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Possible values are: * a subscription number * a subscription ID</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Charge detail</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The segmented rate plan charges. ## Get subscriptions by key and version

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-subscriptions-versions-by-subscription-key-version>`

This REST API reference describes how to retrieve detailed information about a specified
subscription in a specified version. When you create a subscription amendment, you create a new
version of the subscription. You can use this method to retrieve information about a subscription in
any version. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/subscriptions/{subscription-key}/versions/{version} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Subscription number. For example, A-S00000135.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Subscription version. For example, 1.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Charge detail</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The segmented rate plan charges. When an amendment results in a change to a charge, Zuora creates a segmented rate plan charge. Use this field to track segment charges. Possible values are: * <em>last-segment</em>: (Default) The last rate plan charge on the subscription. The last rate plan charge is the last one in the order of time on the subscription rather than the most recent changed charge on the subscription. * <em>current-segment</em>: The segmented charge that is active on todayâs date (effectiveStartDate &lt;= todayâs date &lt; effectiveEndDate). * <em>all-segments</em>: All the segmented charges. The <code>chargeSegments</code> field is returned in the response. The <code>chargeSegments</code> field contains an array of the charge information for all the charge segments. * <em>specific-segment&amp;as-of-date=date</em>: The segmented charge that is active on a date you specified (effectiveStartDate &lt;= specific date &lt; effectiveEndDate). The format of the date is yyyy-mm-dd.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. - MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:TIMEOUT
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:UNAUTHORIZED
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get taxation item

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-taxationitems-by-id>`

Retrieves the information about a specific taxation item by ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /v1/taxationitems/{id} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a taxation item.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Multi-entity: Get entities that a user can access

`<mule-zuora-connector:get-v1-users-accessible-entities-by-username>`

**Note:** The Multi-entity feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Retrieves detailed information about all the
entities that a user has permission to access. ## User Access Permission You can make the call as
any entity user. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/v1/users/{username}/accessible-entities endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specify the login user name that you want to retrieve.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
