If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:BAD_REQUEST
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:TIMEOUT
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:UNAUTHORIZED
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Update refund

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-refunds-by-refund-id>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled. The
Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Updates the basic and finance information about a refund.
This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /v1/refunds/{refundId} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Refund Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The unique ID of a refund. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Cancel refund

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-refunds-cancel-by-refund-id>`

**Note:** This feature is only available if you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled. The
Invoice Settlement feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this feature,
submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Cancels a refund. If you have the Invoice Settlement
feature enabled, refunds applied to credit balance cannot be cancelled. This operation makes an HTTP
PUT request to the /v1/refunds/{refundId}/cancel endpoint

The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Update revenue schedule basic information

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-revenue-schedules-basic-information-by-rs-number>`

Retrieves basic information of a revenue schedule by specifying the revenue schedule number. Request
and response field descriptions and sample code are provided. This operation makes an HTTP PUT
request to the /v1/revenue-schedules/{rs-number}/basic-information endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rs number</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Revenue schedule number. The revenue schedule number is always prefixed with "RS", for example, "RS-00000001".</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

Update the Zuora Revenue accounting code that corresponds to a specific Product Rate Plan Charge Id
in Zuora Billing. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /v1/revpro-accounting-codes
endpoint

## Update Sequence Set

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-sequence-sets-by-id>`

Updates a specific sequence set configured for billing documents, payments, and refunds. Billing
documents include invoices, credit memos, and debit memos.

> [!NOTE] The Credit and Debit Memos feature is available only if you have the Invoice Settlement
> feature enabled. The Invoice Settlement feature is in Limited Availability. If you want access to
> this feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support.

This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /v1/sequence-sets/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

## Update subscription

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-subscriptions-by-subscription-key>`

Use this call to make the following kinds of changes to a subscription: \* Add a note \* Change the
renewal term or auto-renewal flag \* Change the term length or change between evergreen and
termed \* Add a new product rate plan \* Remove an existing subscription rate plan \* Change the
quantity or price of an existing subscription rate plan Notes \* The Update Subscription call
creates a new subscription, which has the old subscription number but a new subscription ID. The old
subscription is canceled but remains in the system. \* In one request, this call can make: \* Up to
9 combined add, update, and remove changes \* No more than 1 change to terms & conditions \* Updates
are performed in the following sequence: 1. First change the notes on the existing subscription, if
requested. 2. Then change the terms and conditions, if requested. 3. Then perform the remaining
amendments based upon the effective dates specified. If multiple amendments have the same
contract-effective dates, then execute adds before updates, and updates before removes. \* The
update operation is atomic. If any of the updates fails, the entire operation is rolled back. \* The
response of the Update Subscription call is based on the REST API minor version you set in the
request header. The response structure might be different if you use different minor version
numbers. \* If you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled, it is best practice to set the
`zuora-version` parameter to `211.0` or later. Otherwise, an error occurs. Override a Tiered Price
There are two ways you override a tiered price: \* Override a specific tier number For example:
`tiers[{tier:1,price:8},{tier:2,price:6}]` \* Override the entire tier structure For example:
`tiers[{tier:1,price:8,startingUnit:1,endingUnit:100,priceFormat:"FlatFee"}, {tier:2,price:6,startingUnit:101,priceFormat:"FlatFee"}]`
If you just override a specific tier, do not include the `startingUnit` field in the request. This
operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /v1/subscriptions/{subscription-key} endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Subscription number or ID.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The minor version of the Zuora REST API. You need to set this parameter if you use the following fields: * collect * invoice * includeExistingDraftDocItems * previewType * runBilling * targetDate If you have the Invoice Settlement feature enabled, you need to specify this parameter. Otherwise, an error is returned. See [Zuora REST API Versions](<a href="https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/">https://developer.zuora.com/v1-api-reference/introduction/</a>) for more information.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Cancel Subscription

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-subscriptions-cancel-by-subscription-key>`

This REST API reference describes how to cancel an active subscription. **Note**: If you have the
Invoice Settlement feature enabled, it is best practice to set the `zuora-version` parameter to
`211.0` or later. Otherwise, an error occurs. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/v1/subscriptions/{subscription-key}/cancel endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Subscription number or ID. Subscription status must be <code>Active</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The minor version of the Zuora REST API. You only need to set this parameter if you use the following fields: * invoice * collect * runBilling * targetDate</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CLIENT_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:CONNECTIVITY
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- MULE-ZUORA-CONNECTOR:NOT_FOUND

## Update Subscription Custom Fields

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-subscriptions-custom-fields-by-subscription-number>`

**Note:** This operation is only available if you have the Orders feature enabled. The migration to
Orders is in **Limited Availability**. We are actively soliciting feedback from a small set of early
adopters before releasing as generally available. Updates the custom fields of a specified
subscription. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/v1/subscriptions/{subscriptionNumber}/customFields endpoint

## Renew subscription

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-subscriptions-renew-by-subscription-key>`

Renews a termed subscription using existing renewal terms. **Note**: If you have the Invoice
Settlement feature enabled, it is best practice to set the `zuora-version` parameter to `211.0` or
later. Otherwise, an error occurs. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/v1/subscriptions/{subscription-key}/renew endpoint

### Parameters

The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. ## Suspend subscription

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-subscriptions-suspend-by-subscription-key>`

This REST API reference describes how to suspend an active subscription. **Note**: If you have the
Invoice Settlement feature enabled, it is best practice to set the `zuora-version` parameter to
`211.0` or later. Otherwise, an error occurs. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/v1/subscriptions/{subscription-key}/suspend endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subscription key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Subscription number or ID. Subscription status must be Active.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The minor version of the Zuora REST API. You only need to set this parameter if you use the following fields: * invoice * collect * runBilling * targetDate</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

Permission You must make the calls as an administrator of the entity that you want to accept the
user access to. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /v1/users/{username}/accept-access
endpoint

## Multi-entity: Deny user access

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-users-deny-access-by-username>`

**Note:** The Multi-entity feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Denies a user access to an entity. ## User Access
Permission You must make the calls as an administrator of the entity that you want to deny the user
access to. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /v1/users/{username}/deny-access endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specify the login name of the user that you want to deny the access.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If you have Zuora Multi-entity enabled and the OAuth token is valid for more than one entity, you must use this header to specify which entity to perform the operation in. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for request to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Multi-entity: Send user access requests

`<mule-zuora-connector:update-v1-users-request-access-by-username>`

**Note:** The Multi-entity feature is in **Limited Availability**. If you want access to this
feature, submit a request to Zuora Global Support. Sends access requests to the entities that a user
wants to access. ## User Access Permission You must make the call as an administrator of the entity,
in which the request user is created. Also, this administrator must have the permission to access
the entities that the request user wants to access. This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the
/v1/users/{username}/request-access endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specify the login name of the user who wants to access other entities.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Track Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A custom identifier for tracing the API call. If you set a value for this header, Zuora returns the same value in the response headers. This header enables you to associate your system process identifiers with Zuora API calls, to assist with troubleshooting in the event of an issue. The value of this field must use the US-ASCII character set and must not include any of the following characters: colon (<code>:</code>), semicolon (<code>;</code>), double quote (<code>"</code>), and quote (<code>'</code>).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Zuora Entity Ids</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An entity ID. If the OAuth token is only valid for a single entity, or you do not have Zuora Multi-entity enabled, you do not need to set this header.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>the content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. ## Update tasks in batch

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Crl File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Http Response Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Status Code</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reason Phrase</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of memory that will be allocated to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can be fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of instances that is initially be allowed to be kept in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of instances that will be kept in memory. If more than that is required, then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Zuora Connector 6.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure an input source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems, as described in
[View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Zuora Connector Reference](zuora-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Zuora Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the connectorâs
namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `zuora` in the search field.
3.  Click **Zuora** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source operation initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of
these input sources to use with Zuora Connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**, which initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host
  and port
- `Scheduler`, which initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP listener, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP listener in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for Zuora Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Zuora** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials the connector uses to access the target Zuora system.

To configure the global element for Zuora Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, enter the following required values to configure authentication for:
    - [**Basic Auth Connection Type**](#basic-auth)
    - [**OAuth2 Connection Provider**](#oauth-authentication)

      You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
      (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
      properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
      configure them, see
      [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

### Basic Authentication

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure Basic authentication:

- **Username**  
  Zuora username
- **Password**  
  Zuora password
- **Base Uri**  
  Base path of the Zuora REST API for your instance, for example:
  `[https://apisandbox-api.zuora.com:443/](https://apisandbox-api.zuora.com:443/)`.

The following screenshot shows an example of configuring Basic authentication:

![Global element properties window for Basic authentication](_images/zuora-5-basic-auth-studio.png)

### OAuth 2.0

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure OAuth 2.0 authentication:

- **Client id**  
  ID of the client
- **Client secret**  
  Client secret used to authenticate the client.
- **Token URL**  
  Service providerâs accessToken endpoint URL, for example:
  `https://rest.apisandbox.zuora.com/oauth/token`
- **Scopes**  
  A comma-separated list of supported OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. If not
  provided, it defaults to the OAuth scopes in the annotation

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## Next Step

After configuring an app in Studio, see the [Zuora Examples](zuora-connector-examples) section to
experiment with the Zuora connector.

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating Zuora Connector to 6.0.x

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for Zuora (Zuora Connector) to version 6.0.x.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>5.x.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>6.0.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes in This Release

This release contains new and changed operations, metadata, fields, and values.

## New Operations

Zuora Connector 6.0.0 includes the following new operations:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="2">New in Zuora 6.0.0</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Description</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Generate an OAuth token</p></td><td><p>Generates a bearer token that enables an OAuth client to authenticate with the Zuora REST API.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object POSTFeature</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /v1/object/feature endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get workflow task usage</p></td><td><p>Gets workflow task usage sorted by day within a specified time frame.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object PUTFeature</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /v1/object/feature/{id} endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>PUT Rev Pro Accounting Codes</p></td><td><p>Update the Zuora Revenue accounting code that corresponds to a specific Product Rate Plan Charge Id in Zuora Billing.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update subscription custom fields of a specified subscription version</p></td><td><p>Updates the custom fields of a specified subscription version.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Removed Operations

The following operations were removed in Zuora Connector 6.0.0:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="2">Removed in Zuora 6.0.0</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Description</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Import usage file</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /usage-imports endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create usage records</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /usage endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request breakdown of credit memo items by order</p></td><td><p>Retrieves specified credit memo items which are broken down by orders.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request breakdown of invoice items by order</p></td><td><p>Retrieves the specified invoice items which are broken down by orders.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delete schema of Usage custom field definition</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /fields/definitions/Usage endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CRUD: Delete payment method snapshot</p></td><td><p>This REST API reference describes how to delete a Payment Method Snapshot.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get Usage custom field definition</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /fields/definitions/Usage endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get rating results by account</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rating/rated-results/account/{accountNumber} endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get rating results by charge</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rating/rated-results/charge/{chargeNumber} endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get rating results by subscription</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rating/rated-results/subscription/{subscriptionNumber} endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get rated usage by account</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rating/rated-usages/account/{accountNumber} endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get rated usage by charge</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rating/rated-usages/charge/{chargeNumber} endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get rated usage by subscription</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /rating/rated-usages/subscription/{subscriptionNumber} endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get status of usage import</p></td><td><p>Get details of usage import</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Download usage import failure file</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /usage-imports/{id}/errors endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Download usage import file</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /usage-imports/{id}/import-file endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get status of usage import</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /usage-imports/{id}/status endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Download usage file template</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /usage-imports/templates/html endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query usage records in stream</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /usage/stream-query endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get breakdown of credit memo by order</p></td><td><p>Retrieves a specified credit memo that is broken down by orders. One credit memo item might be broken down into a list of order related items.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get breakdown of invoice by order</p></td><td><p>Retrieves a specified invoice that is broken down by orders. One invoice item might be broken down into a list of order related items.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get rated result for order</p></td><td><p>Retrieves the rated results of all the subscriptions in the specified order.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Set schema of Usage custom field definition</p></td><td><p>This operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /fields/definitions/Usage endpoint</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update usage record</p></td><td><p>Updates certain information of a specified usage record.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes in Operations Parameters

The following changes were made to these operations:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th colspan="3">Existing in Zuora 5.x</th><th colspan="3">Modified in Zuora 6.x</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Operation ## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, you must:

1.  Create a backup of your files, data, and configuration in case you need to restore to the
    previous version.
2.  Install Zuora Connector to replace the Zuora operations that were previously included in Zuora
    Connector 5.x.x.

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to perform the upgrade to Zuora Connector 6.0.0:

1.  In Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the Mule Palette view, click **Search in Exchange**.
3.  In Add Modules to Project, type 'Zuora' in the search field.
4.  In Available modules, select **Zuora Connector**, and then click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.
6.  Verify that the `zuora-connector` dependency version is `6.0.0` in the `pom.xml`.

Anypoint Studio upgrades the connector automatically.

## Verify the Upgrade

After you install the latest version of the connector, follow these steps to verify the upgrade:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, verify that there are no errors in the **Problems** or **Console** views.
    - If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
      console window.
    - If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
      console.

      Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view
      the app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

2.  Check the project `pom.xml` and verify that there are no problems.
3.  Test the connection and verify that the operations work.

## Troubleshooting

If there are problems with caching the parameters and caching the metadata, try restarting Anypoint
Studio.

## Reverting the Upgrade

If it is necessary to revert to the previous version of Zuora Connector, change the
`zuora-connector` dependency version `6.0.0` in the projectâs `pom.xml` to the previous version.

You must update the projectâs `pom.xml` in Anypoint Studio.

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Zuora XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you manually code a Mule app in XML either from the Studio XML editor or a text editor, you must
perform the following actions to get access to the connector:

1.  Add the namespace for the connector to your XML flow.
2.  Add dependency information to your Maven `pom.xml` file.

In the Studio visual editor, when you drag a connector from the palette to the canvas, Studio
automatically populates the XML code with the connector namespace and schema location.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

If you manually code the Mule app using Studioâs XML editor or another text editor, paste the
following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML.

```text
xsi:schemaLocation="
...
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/zuora-connector
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/zuora-connector/current/mule-zuora-connector.xsd"
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the mule XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
      xmlns:mule-zuora-connector="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-zuora-connector"
	    xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	    xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
      xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
      xsi:schemaLocation="
               http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
               http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
               http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/mule-zuora-connector
               http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/ mule/mule-zuora-connector/current/mule-mule-zuora-connector.xsd
               http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd">
      <!-- Insert your configuration elements and your flow here -->
</mule>
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The `pom.xml` Maven file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app. When you
code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to this
connector.

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-zuora-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information, access the connector in
[Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/) and click **Dependency Snippets**.

## Next Step

After completing this section, see the [Zuora Examples](zuora-connector-examples) to experiment with
Zuora Connector.
